description: Comparing an indexed string column to a numeric literal forces a full scan via implicit conversion.
body: Failure mode: a WHERE clause comparing a VARCHAR column to an integer literal (or mismatched collations across a join) triggers implicit type conversion, which prevents the index from being used and falls back to a full clustered scan. Detection signals: EXPLAIN shows type ALL and no key on a column that is indexed, and the slow query log captures the statement. Do not use loose, mismatched-type comparisons in hot predicates. Safer fallback: quote the literal to match the column type, align collations across joined columns, and confirm EXPLAIN reports a ref access using the intended index.
